response.justification: While the resume demonstrates strong full-stack engineering fundamentals and proven ability to architect complex systems, it lacks the core technical requirements for this role. Java and Kotlin expertise are minimally evidenced, Spring ecosystem and enterprise frameworks are entirely absent, and IDE tooling experience is limited to a single role 25 years ago. The candidate's recent focus on TypeScript/Node.js and AI infrastructure represents a significant domain mismatch for a mid-senior position that requires immediate productivity in JVM ecosystem tooling.
strengths: Proven track record architecting and maintaining large-scale systems handling millions of concurrent operations with emphasis on clean code, testing, and software craftsmanship
Extensive cross-platform and polyglot programming experience demonstrating strong learning ability and technical adaptability across diverse domains
15+ years at current role with demonstrated ability to contribute across full technology stack from infrastructure to product work
concerns: Java listed in skills but with no depth—no Kotlin experience despite both being core requirements; Java exposure limited to brief mentions in roles 15+ years ago
Complete absence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise framework experience, which are central technical focus areas for the role

Minimal IDE tooling background with last IDE-related work (LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments) 25 years ago; current focus is TypeScript/Node.js backend and AI infrastructure, not JVM language tooling

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling background
